paper_title: Analysis of Pathological Stones from Al Khiday Cemetery

methods.description: This method involves using X-ray computed micro-tomography to investigate the internal microstructure of undamaged stones S1 and S2. It provides a non-destructive way to visualize the internal features of the stones.

steps.description: X-ray micro-tomographic scans were performed at the Department of Geosciences (University of Padua, Italy) using a Skyscan 1172 high-resolution X-mCT scanner (Bruker).
steps.searchable_summary: perform X-ray micro-tomographic scans using Skyscan 1172

steps.description: The samples were irradiated with a polychromatic X-ray cone beam, filtered with 0.5 mm aluminium foil.
steps.searchable_summary: irradiate samples with X-ray cone beam filtered with aluminium foil

steps.description: For each sample, 720 radiographs were acquired over a rotation of 360˚ with a step of 0.5˚ and an exposure time of 1000 ms for each projection.
Three-dimensional assemblages of cross-sectional slices were obtained by tomographic reconstruction using a filtered back-projection algorithm, with a final voxel resolution of 27 μm³.
steps.searchable_summary: perform tomographic reconstruction using filtered back-projection

methods.method_name: Mineralogical Analysis by X-ray Powder Diffraction (XRPD)
methods.description: This method uses X-ray powder diffraction to determine the crystallographic composition of the stones. It involves grinding stone fragments into powder and analyzing them using a diffractometer.

steps.description: Mineralogical analysis was performed by X-ray powder diffraction (XRPD) on the powders produced from grinding a fragment of the broken stone S3 and on that obtained from the micro-drilling of a small portion of the complete stones (stone S1 and stone S2).
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRPD on ground stone powders

steps.description: The finely ground powders were measured using an X'Pert PRO diffractometer (PANalytical) in Bragg-Brentano geometry, equipped with a Cu-anode X-ray tube (40 kV and 40 mA) and a X'Celerator detector.
steps.searchable_summary: measure powders using X'Pert PRO diffractometer

steps.description: Diffractograms were acquired in the 3˚-80˚2θ range, with a step size of 0.02˚ and scanning time corresponding to 1 s counting time per step.
The quantitative analysis was performed by Rietveld refinement, using the MAUD program.

methods.description: This method uses time-of-flight neutron powder diffraction to analyze the crystallographic composition of stone S2. It provides high-resolution data on the atomic structure.

steps.description: The non-invasive ToF-NPD diffraction measurements on stone S2 were performed at the Italian Neutron Experimental Station (INES) at the ISIS Facility (UK), by using the time of flight (ToF) technique.
The collected patterns were analyzed by the Rietveld method, using the GSAS package.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze patterns using Rietveld method with GSAS

methods.method_name: Microstructural Analysis by Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM)
methods.description: This method involves using scanning electron microscopy to study the microstructure of stone fragments. It provides detailed images and chemical composition data.

steps.description: Microstructural analysis was performed on some fragments of the broken stone S3 by scanning electron microscopy (SEM) using a JSM Jeol 6490 equipped with a tungsten electron source and an EDAX energy dispersive X-ray detector (EDS) for micro-chemical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform SEM analysis using JSM Jeol 6490

steps.description: A small portion of the stone was also analysed by SEM without any preparation (but gold-coated), in order to perform secondary electron images (SEM-SE) and have morphological information on the crystals constituting the stone.
steps.searchable_summary: perform SEM-SE imaging on gold-coated stone
